question: Case Study - A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company needs to use the central model registry to manage different versions of models in the application. Which action will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: Create a separate Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository for each model.
choices.B: Use Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) and unique tags for each model version.
choices.C: Use the SageMaker Model Registry and model groups to catalog the models.
choices.D: Use the SageMaker Model Registry and unique tags for each model version.

question: Case Study - A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company is experimenting with consecutive training jobs. How can the company MINIMIZE infrastructure startup times for these jobs?

choices.A: Use Managed Spot Training.
choices.B: Use SageMaker managed warm pools.
choices.C: Use SageMaker Training Compiler.
choices.D: Use the SageMaker distributed data parallelism (SMDDP) library.

question: Case Study - A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company must implement a manual approval-based workflow to ensure that only approved models can be deployed to production endpoints. Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: Use SageMaker Experiments to facilitate the approval process during model registration.
choices.B: Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking on the central model registry. Create tracking entities for the approval process.
choices.C: Use SageMaker Model Monitor to evaluate the performance of the model and to manage the approval.
choices.D: Use SageMaker Pipelines. When a model version is registered, use the AWS SDK to change the approval status to "Approved."

question: Case Study - A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company needs to run an on-demand workflow to monitor bias drift for models that are deployed to real-time endpoints from the application. Which action will meet this requirement?

choices.A: Configure the application to invoke an AWS Lambda function that runs a SageMaker Clarify job.
choices.B: Invoke an AWS Lambda function to pull the sagemaker-model-monitor-analyzer built-in SageMaker image.
choices.C: Use AWS Glue Data Quality to monitor bias.
choices.D: Use SageMaker notebooks to compare the bias.

question: Case study - An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. Which AWS service or feature can aggregate the data from the various data sources?

choices.A: Amazon EMR Spark jobs
choices.B: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
choices.C: Amazon DynamoDB
choices.D: AWS Lake Formation

question: Case study - An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. After the data is aggregated, the ML engineer must implement a solution to automatically detect anomalies in the data and to visualize the result. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Use Amazon Athena to automatically detect the anomalies and to visualize the result.
choices.B: Use Amazon Redshift Spectrum to automatically detect the anomalies. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the result.
choices.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to automatically detect the anomalies and to visualize the result.
choices.D: Use AWS Batch to automatically detect the anomalies. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the result.

question: Case study - An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. The training dataset includes categorical data and numerical data. The ML engineer must prepare the training dataset to maximize the accuracy of the model. Which action will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: Use AWS Glue to transform the categorical data into numerical data.
choices.B: Use AWS Glue to transform the numerical data into categorical data.
choices.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to transform the categorical data into numerical data.
choices.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to transform the numerical data into categorical data.

question: Case study - An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. Before the ML engineer trains the model, the ML engineer must resolve the issue of the imbalanced data. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational effort?

choices.A: Use Amazon Athena to identify patterns that contribute to the imbalance. Adjust the dataset accordingly.
choices.B: Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic built-in algorithms to process the imbalanced dataset.
choices.C: Use AWS Glue DataBrew built-in features to oversample the minority class.
choices.D: Use the Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler balance data operation to oversample the minority class.

question: Case study - An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. The ML engineer needs to use an Amazon SageMaker built-in algorithm to train the model. Which algorithm should the ML engineer use to meet this requirement?

choices.A: LightGBM
choices.B: Linear learner
choices.C: К-means clustering
choices.D: Neural Topic Model (NTM)

question: A company has deployed an XGBoost prediction model in production to predict if a customer is likely to cancel a subscription. The company uses Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor to detect deviations in the F1 score. During a baseline analysis of model quality, the company recorded a threshold for the F1 score. After several months of no change, the model's F1 score decreases significantly. What could be the reason for the reduced F1 score?

choices.A: Concept drift occurred in the underlying customer data that was used for predictions.
choices.B: The model was not sufficiently complex to capture all the patterns in the original baseline data.
choices.C: The original baseline data had a data quality issue of missing values.
choices.D: Incorrect ground truth labels were provided to Model Monitor during the calculation of the baseline.

question: A company has a team of data scientists who use Amazon SageMaker notebook instances to test ML models. When the data scientists need new permissions, the company attaches the permissions to each individual role that was created during the creation of the SageMaker notebook instance. The company needs to centralize management of the team's permissions. Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: Create a single IAM role that has the necessary permissions. Attach the role to each notebook instance that the team uses.
choices.B: Create a single IAM group. Add the data scientists to the group. Associate the group with each notebook instance that the team uses.
choices.C: Create a single IAM user. Attach the AdministratorAccess AWS managed IAM policy to the user. Configure each notebook instance to use the IAM user.
choices.D: Create a single IAM group. Add the data scientists to the group. Create an IAM role. Attach the AdministratorAccess AWS managed IAM policy to the role. Associate the role with the group. Associate the group with each notebook instance that the team uses.

question: An ML engineer needs to use an ML model to predict the price of apartments in a specific location. Which metric should the ML engineer use to evaluate the model's performance?

choices.A: Accuracy
choices.B: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
choices.C: F1 score
choices.D: Mean absolute error (MAE)

question: An ML engineer has trained a neural network by using stochastic gradient descent (SGD). The neural network performs poorly on the test set. The values for training loss and validation loss remain high and show an oscillating pattern. The values decrease for a few epochs and then increase for a few epochs before repeating the same cycle. What should the ML engineer do to improve the training process?

choices.A: Introduce early stopping.
choices.B: Increase the size of the test set.
choices.C: Increase the learning rate.
choices.D: Decrease the learning rate.

question: An ML engineer needs to process thousands of existing CSV objects and new CSV objects that are uploaded. The CSV objects are stored in a central Amazon S3 bucket and have the same number of columns. One of the columns is a transaction date. The ML engineer must query the data based on the transaction date. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: Use an Amazon Athena CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) statement to create a table based on the transaction date from data in the central S3 bucket. Query the objects from the table.
choices.B: Create a new S3 bucket for processed data. Set up S3 replication from the central S3 bucket to the new S3 bucket. Use S3 Object Lambda to query the objects based on transaction date.
choices.C: Create a new S3 bucket for processed data. Use AWS Glue for Apache Spark to create a job to query the CSV objects based on transaction date. Configure the job to store the results in the new S3 bucket. Query the objects from the new S3 bucket.
choices.D: Create a new S3 bucket for processed data. Use Amazon Data Firehose to transfer the data from the central S3 bucket to the new S3 bucket. Configure Firehose to run an AWS Lambda function to query the data based on transaction date.

question: A company has a large, unstructured dataset. The dataset includes many duplicate records across several key attributes. Which solution on AWS will detect duplicates in the dataset with the LEAST code development?

choices.A: Use Amazon Mechanical Turk jobs to detect duplicates.
choices.B: Use Amazon QuickSight ML Insights to build a custom deduplication model.
choices.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to pre-process and detect duplicates.
choices.D: Use the AWS Glue FindMatches transform to detect duplicates.

question: A company needs to run a batch data-processing job on Amazon EC2 instances. The job will run during the weekend and will take 90 minutes to finish running. The processing can handle interruptions. The company will run the job every weekend for the next 6 months. Which EC2 instance purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

choices.A: Spot Instances
choices.B: Reserved Instances
choices.C: On-Demand Instances
choices.D: Dedicated Instances

question: An ML engineer has an Amazon Comprehend custom model in Account A in the us-east-1 Region. The ML engineer needs to copy the model to Account В in the same Region. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?

choices.A: Use Amazon S3 to make a copy of the model. Transfer the copy to Account B.
choices.B: Create a resource-based IAM policy. Use the Amazon Comprehend ImportModel API operation to copy the model to Account B.
choices.C: Use AWS DataSync to replicate the model from Account A to Account B.
choices.D: Create an AWS Site-to-Site VPN connection between Account A and Account В to transfer the model.

question: An ML engineer is training a simple neural network model. The ML engineer tracks the performance of the model over time on a validation dataset. The model's performance improves substantially at first and then degrades after a specific number of epochs. Which solutions will mitigate this problem? (Choose two.)

choices.A: Enable early stopping on the model.
choices.B: Increase dropout in the layers.
choices.C: Increase the number of layers.
choices.D: Increase the number of neurons.
choices.E: Investigate and reduce the sources of model bias.

question: A company has a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) application that uses a vector database to store embeddings of documents. The company must migrate the application to AWS and must implement a solution that provides semantic search of text files. The company has already migrated the text repository to an Amazon S3 bucket. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Use an AWS Batch job to process the files and generate embeddings. Use AWS Glue to store the embeddings. Use SQL queries to perform the semantic searches.
choices.B: Use a custom Amazon SageMaker notebook to run a custom script to generate embeddings. Use SageMaker Feature Store to store the embeddings. Use SQL queries to perform the semantic searches.
choices.C: Use the Amazon Kendra S3 connector to ingest the documents from the S3 bucket into Amazon Kendra. Query Amazon Kendra to perform the semantic searches.
choices.D: Use an Amazon Textract asynchronous job to ingest the documents from the S3 bucket. Query Amazon Textract to perform the semantic searches.

question: A company uses Amazon Athena to query a dataset in Amazon S3. The dataset has a target variable that the company wants to predict. The company needs to use the dataset in a solution to determine if a model can predict the target variable. Which solution will provide this information with the LEAST development effort?

choices.A: Create a new model by using Amazon SageMaker Autopilot. Report the model's achieved performance.
choices.B: Implement custom scripts to perform data pre-processing, multiple linear regression, and performance evaluation. Run the scripts on Amazon EC2 instances.
choices.C: Configure Amazon Macie to analyze the dataset and to create a model. Report the model's achieved performance.
choices.D: Select a model from Amazon Bedrock. Tune the model with the data. Report the model's achieved performance.

question: A company wants to predict the success of advertising campaigns by considering the color scheme of each advertisement. An ML engineer is preparing data for a neural network model. The dataset includes color information as categorical data. Which technique for feature engineering should the ML engineer use for the model?

choices.A: Apply label encoding to the color categories. Automatically assign each color a unique integer.
choices.B: Implement padding to ensure that all color feature vectors have the same length.
choices.C: Perform dimensionality reduction on the color categories.
choices.D: One-hot encode the color categories to transform the color scheme feature into a binary matrix.

question: A company uses a hybrid cloud environment. A model that is deployed on premises uses data in Amazon 53 to provide customers with a live conversational engine. The model is using sensitive data. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to identify and remove the sensitive data. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: Deploy the model on Amazon SageMaker. Create a set of AWS Lambda functions to identify and remove the sensitive data.
choices.B: Deploy the model on an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster that uses AWS Fargate. Create an AWS Batch job to identify and remove the sensitive data.
choices.C: Use Amazon Macie to identify the sensitive data. Create a set of AWS Lambda functions to remove the sensitive data.
choices.D: Use Amazon Comprehend to identify the sensitive data. Launch Amazon EC2 instances to remove the sensitive data.

question: An ML engineer needs to create data ingestion pipelines and ML model deployment pipelines on AWS. All the raw data is stored in Amazon S3 buckets. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Use Amazon Data Firehose to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines.
choices.B: Use AWS Glue to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines.
choices.C: Use Amazon Redshift ML to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines.
choices.D: Use Amazon Athena to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use an Amazon SageMaker notebook to create the model deployment pipelines.

question: A company that has hundreds of data scientists is using Amazon SageMaker to create ML models. The models are in model groups in the SageMaker Model Registry. The data scientists are grouped into three categories: computer vision, natural language processing (NLP), and speech recognition. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to organize the existing models into these groups to improve model discoverability at scale. The solution must not affect the integrity of the model artifacts and their existing groupings. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Create a custom tag for each of the three categories. Add the tags to the model packages in the SageMaker Model Registry.
choices.B: Create a model group for each category. Move the existing models into these category model groups.
choices.C: Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking to automatically identify and tag which model groups should contain the models.
choices.D: Create a Model Registry collection for each of the three categories. Move the existing model groups into the collections.

question: A company runs an Amazon SageMaker domain in a public subnet of a newly created VPC. The network is configured properly, and ML engineers can access the SageMaker domain. Recently, the company discovered suspicious traffic to the domain from a specific IP address. The company needs to block traffic from the specific IP address. Which update to the network configuration will meet this requirement?

choices.A: Create a security group inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the security group to the domain.
choices.B: Create a network ACL inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the rule to the default network Ad for the subnet where the domain is located.
choices.C: Create a shadow variant for the domain. Configure SageMaker Inference Recommender to send traffic from the specific IP address to the shadow endpoint.
choices.D: Create a VPC route table to deny inbound traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the route table to the domain.

question: A company is gathering audio, video, and text data in various languages. The company needs to use a large language model (LLM) to summarize the gathered data that is in Spanish. Which solution will meet these requirements in the LEAST amount of time?

choices.A: Train and deploy a model in Amazon SageMaker to convert the data into English text. Train and deploy an LLM in SageMaker to summarize the text.
choices.B: Use Amazon Transcribe and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Jurassic model to summarize the text.
choices.C: Use Amazon Rekognition and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Anthropic Claude model to summarize the text.
choices.D: Use Amazon Comprehend and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Stable Diffusion model to summarize the text.

question: A financial company receives a high volume of real-time market data streams from an external provider. The streams consist of thousands of JSON records every second. The company needs to implement a scalable solution on AWS to identify anomalous data points. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: Ingest real-time data into Amazon Kinesis data streams. Use the built-in RANDOM_CUT_FOREST function in Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to process the data streams and to detect data anomalies.
choices.B: Ingest real-time data into Amazon Kinesis data streams. Deploy an Amazon SageMaker endpoint for real-time outlier detection. Create an AWS Lambda function to detect anomalies. Use the data streams to invoke the Lambda function.
choices.C: Ingest real-time data into Apache Kafka on Amazon EC2 instances. Deploy an Amazon SageMaker endpoint for real-time outlier detection. Create an AWS Lambda function to detect anomalies. Use the data streams to invoke the Lambda function.
choices.D: Send real-time data to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queue. Create an AWS Lambda function to consume the queue messages. Program the Lambda function to start an AWS Glue extract, transform, and load (ETL) job for batch processing and anomaly detection.

question: A company has a large collection of chat recordings from customer interactions after a product release. An ML engineer needs to create an ML model to analyze the chat data. The ML engineer needs to determine the success of the product by reviewing customer sentiments about the product. Which action should the ML engineer take to complete the evaluation in the LEAST amount of time?

choices.A: Use Amazon Rekognition to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations.
choices.B: Train a Naive Bayes classifier to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations.
choices.C: Use Amazon Comprehend to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations.
choices.D: Use random forests to classify sentiments of the chat conversations.

question: A company has a conversational AI assistant that sends requests through Amazon Bedrock to an Anthropic Claude large language model (LLM). Users report that when they ask similar questions multiple times, they sometimes receive different answers. An ML engineer needs to improve the responses to be more consistent and less random. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Increase the temperature parameter and the top_k parameter.
choices.B: Increase the temperature parameter. Decrease the top_k parameter.
choices.C: Decrease the temperature parameter. Increase the top_k parameter.
choices.D: Decrease the temperature parameter and the top_k parameter.

question: A company is using ML to predict the presence of a specific weed in a farmer's field. The company is using the Amazon SageMaker linear learner built-in algorithm with a value of multiclass_dassifier for the predictorjype hyperparameter. What should the company do to MINIMIZE false positives?

choices.A: Set the value of the weight decay hyperparameter to zero.
choices.B: Increase the number of training epochs.
choices.C: Increase the value of the target_precision hyperparameter.
choices.D: Change the value of the predictorjype hyperparameter to regressor.

question: A company has implemented a data ingestion pipeline for sales transactions from its ecommerce website. The company uses Amazon Data Firehose to ingest data into Amazon OpenSearch Service. The buffer interval of the Firehose stream is set for 60 seconds. An OpenSearch linear model generates real-time sales forecasts based on the data and presents the data in an OpenSearch dashboard. The company needs to optimize the data ingestion pipeline to support sub-second latency for the real-time dashboard. Which change to the architecture will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Use zero buffering in the Firehose stream. Tune the batch size that is used in the PutRecordBatch operation.
choices.B: Replace the Firehose stream with an AWS DataSync task. Configure the task with enhanced fan-out consumers.
choices.C: Increase the buffer interval of the Firehose stream from 60 seconds to 120 seconds.
choices.D: Replace the Firehose stream with an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.

question: A company has trained an ML model in Amazon SageMaker. The company needs to host the model to provide inferences in a production environment. The model must be highly available and must respond with minimum latency. The size of each request will be between 1 KB and 3 MB. The model will receive unpredictable bursts of requests during the day. The inferences must adapt proportionally to the changes in demand. How should the company deploy the model into production to meet these requirements?

choices.A: Create a SageMaker real-time inference endpoint. Configure auto scaling. Configure the endpoint to present the existing model.
choices.B: Deploy the model on an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster. Use ECS scheduled scaling that is based on the CPU of the ECS cluster.
choices.C: Install SageMaker Operator on an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. Deploy the model in Amazon EKS. Set horizontal pod auto scaling to scale replicas based on the memory metric.
choices.D: Use Spot Instances with a Spot Fleet behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) for inferences. Use the ALBRequestCountPerTarget metric as the metric for auto scaling.

question: An ML engineer needs to use an Amazon EMR cluster to process large volumes of data in batches. Any data loss is unacceptable. Which instance purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

choices.A: Run the primary node, core nodes, and task nodes on On-Demand Instances.
choices.B: Run the primary node, core nodes, and task nodes on Spot Instances.
choices.C: Run the primary node on an On-Demand Instance. Run the core nodes and task nodes on Spot Instances.
choices.D: Run the primary node and core nodes on On-Demand Instances. Run the task nodes on Spot Instances.

question: A company wants to improve the sustainability of its ML operations. Which actions will reduce the energy usage and computational resources that are associated with the company's training jobs? (Choose two.)

choices.A: Use Amazon SageMaker Debugger to stop training jobs when non-converging conditions are detected.
choices.B: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth for data labeling.
choices.C: Deploy models by using AWS Lambda functions.
choices.D: Use AWS Trainium instances for training.
choices.E: Use PyTorch or TensorFlow with the distributed training option.

question: A company is planning to create several ML prediction models. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The entire dataset is more than 5 ТВ in size and consists of CSV, JSON, Apache Parquet, and simple text files. The data must be processed in several consecutive steps. The steps include complex manipulations that can take hours to finish running. Some of the processing involves natural language processing (NLP) transformations. The entire process must be automated. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Process data at each step by using Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler. Automate the process by using Data Wrangler jobs.
choices.B: Use Amazon SageMaker notebooks for each data processing step. Automate the process by using Amazon EventBridge.
choices.C: Process data at each step by using AWS Lambda functions. Automate the process by using AWS Step Functions and Amazon EventBridge.
choices.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Pipelines to create a pipeline of data processing steps. Automate the pipeline by using Amazon EventBridge.

question: An ML engineer needs to use AWS CloudFormation to create an ML model that an Amazon SageMaker endpoint will host. Which resource should the ML engineer declare in the CloudFormation template to meet this requirement?

choices.A: AWS::SageMaker::Model
choices.B: AWS::SageMaker::Endpoint
choices.C: AWS::SageMaker::NotebookInstance
choices.D: AWS::SageMaker::Pipeline

question: An advertising company uses AWS Lake Formation to manage a data lake. The data lake contains structured data and unstructured data. The company's ML engineers are assigned to specific advertisement campaigns. The ML engineers must interact with the data through Amazon Athena and by browsing the data directly in an Amazon S3 bucket. The ML engineers must have access to only the resources that are specific to their assigned advertisement campaigns. Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST operationally efficient way?

choices.A: Configure IAM policies on an AWS Glue Data Catalog to restrict access to Athena based on the ML engineers' campaigns.
choices.B: Store users and campaign information in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Configure DynamoDB Streams to invoke an AWS Lambda function to update S3 bucket policies.
choices.C: Use Lake Formation to authorize AWS Glue to access the S3 bucket. Configure Lake Formation tags to map ML engineers to their campaigns.
choices.D: Configure S3 bucket policies to restrict access to the S3 bucket based on the ML engineers' campaigns.

question: An ML engineer needs to use data with Amazon SageMaker Canvas to train an ML model. The data is stored in Amazon S3 and is complex in structure. The ML engineer must use a file format that minimizes processing time for the data. Which file format will meet these requirements?

choices.A: CSV files compressed with Snappy
choices.B: JSON objects in JSONL format
choices.C: JSON files compressed with gzip
choices.D: Apache Parquet files

question: An ML engineer is evaluating several ML models and must choose one model to use in production. The cost of false negative predictions by the models is much higher than the cost of false positive predictions. Which metric finding should the ML engineer prioritize the MOST when choosing the model?

choices.A: Low precision
choices.B: High precision
choices.C: Low recall
choices.D: High recall

question: A company has trained and deployed an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker. The company needs to implement a solution to record and monitor all the API call events for the SageMaker endpoint. The solution also must provide a notification when the number of API call events breaches a threshold. Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: Use SageMaker Debugger to track the inferences and to report metrics. Create a custom rule to provide a notification when the threshold is breached.
choices.B: Use SageMaker Debugger to track the inferences and to report metrics. Use the tensor_variance built-in rule to provide a notification when the threshold is breached.
choices.C: Log all the endpoint invocation API events by using AWS CloudTrail. Use an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard for monitoring. Set up a CloudWatch alarm to provide notification when the threshold is breached.
choices.D: Add the Invocations metric to an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard for monitoring. Set up a CloudWatch alarm to provide notification when the threshold is breached.

question: A company has AWS Glue data processing jobs that are orchestrated by an AWS Glue workflow. The AWS Glue jobs can run on a schedule or can be launched manually. The company is developing pipelines in Amazon SageMaker Pipelines for ML model development. The pipelines will use the output of the AWS Glue jobs during the data processing phase of model development. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution that integrates the AWS Glue jobs with the pipelines. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: Use AWS Step Functions for orchestration of the pipelines and the AWS Glue jobs.
choices.B: Use processing steps in SageMaker Pipelines. Configure inputs that point to the Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the AWS Glue jobs.
choices.C: Use Callback steps in SageMaker Pipelines to start the AWS Glue workflow and to stop the pipelines until the AWS Glue jobs finish running.
choices.D: Use Amazon EventBridge to invoke the pipelines and the AWS Glue jobs in the desired order.

question: A company is using an Amazon Redshift database as its single data source. Some of the data is sensitive. A data scientist needs to use some of the sensitive data from the database. An ML engineer must give the data scientist access to the data without transforming the source data and without storing anonymized data in the database. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST implementation effort?

choices.A: Configure dynamic data masking policies to control how sensitive data is shared with the data scientist at query time.
choices.B: Create a materialized view with masking logic on top of the database. Grant the necessary read permissions to the data scientist.
choices.C: Unload the Amazon Redshift data to Amazon S3. Use Amazon Athena to create schema-on-read with masking logic. Share the view with the data scientist.
choices.D: Unload the Amazon Redshift data to Amazon S3. Create an AWS Glue job to anonymize the data. Share the dataset with the data scientist.

question: An ML engineer is using a training job to fine-tune a deep learning model in Amazon SageMaker Studio. The ML engineer previously used the same pre-trained model with a similar dataset. question: A company has an ML model in Amazon SageMaker AI. An ML engineer needs to implement a monitoring solution to automatically detect changes in the input data distribution of model features.Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.B: B. Configure SageMaker Model Monitor. Establish a model quality baseline. Ensure that the comparison_method option is set to Robust in the baseline constraints file. Configure an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to notify the company about changes in model quality metrics.
choices.C: C. Use SageMaker Debugger with custom rules to track shifts in feature distributions. Configure Amazon CloudWatch alarms to notify the company when the rules detect significant changes.
choices.D: D. Use Amazon CloudWatch to directly observe the SageMaker AI endpoint’s performance metrics. Manually analyze the CloudWatch logs for indicators of data drift or shifts in feature distribution.

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to deploy a new recommendation model for its ecommerce website. The model must use data from all client website interactions as input.Traffic is variable throughout the day. The company needs to create an inference endpoint for the model.Which type of inference endpoint will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

choices.A: A. Batch transform inference endpoint
choices.B: B. Asynchronous inference endpoint
choices.C: C. Real-time inference endpoint
choices.D: D. Serverless inference endpoint

question: A company runs an Amazon SageMaker AI domain in a public subnet of a newly created VPC. The network is configured properly, and ML engineers can access the SageMaker AI domain.Recently, the company discovered suspicious traffic to the domain from a specific IP address. The company needs to block traffic from the specific IP address.Which update to the network configuration will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Create a security group inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the security group to the domain.
choices.B: B. Create a network ACL inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the rule to the default network ACL for the subnet where the domain is located.
choices.C: C. Create a shadow variant for the domain. Configure SageMaker Inference Recommender to send traffic from the specific IP address to the shadow endpoint.
choices.D: D. Create a VPC route table to deny inbound traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the route table to the domain.

question: A company’s ML engineer is creating a classification model. The ML engineer explores the dataset and notices a column that is named day_of_week. The column’s data consists of the following values: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.Which technique should the ML engineer use to convert this column’s data to binary values?

choices.A: A. Binary encoding
choices.B: B. Label encoding
choices.C: C. One-hot encoding Most Voted
choices.D: D. Tokenization

question: An ML engineer wants to use Amazon SageMaker AI to prepare data for training. During exploratory data analysis, the ML engineer notices that several categorical features are missing values.How can the ML engineer use SageMaker AI to solve this problem?

choices.A: A. Use SageMaker Clarify to impute categorical features with the mean value.
choices.B: B. Use SageMaker Clarity to impute categorical features with the mode value.
choices.C: C. Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to impute categorical features with the mean value.
choices.D: D. Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to impute categorical features with the mode value.

question: A company stores user clickstream data in an Amazon S3 bucket in AWS Account A. The company needs to use the data to train an ML model in Amazon SageMaker AI in AWS Account B. The training will take 10 days.The company needs to use only private IP addresses in the training. The company also must make sure that no training metadata is shared with AWS.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Set up VPC peering between Account A and Account B. Contact AWS by email to opt out of metadata collection.
choices.B: B. Set up a VPC endpoint for the S3 bucket. Set the SageMaker AI OPT_OUT_TRACKING environment variable to 1 in the training job.
choices.C: C. Configure a security group policy that is assigned to the S3 bucket in Account A to allow access from only Account B. Create AI services opt-out policies.
choices.D: D. Generate presigned URLs with expiration times for the objects that are stored in the S3 bucket. Access the data by using the presigned URLs. Set the SageMaker AI OPT_OUT_TRACKING environment variable to 1 in the training job.

question: A music streaming company constantly streams song ratings from an application to an Amazon S3 bucket. The company wants to use the ratings as an input for training and inference of an Amazon SageMaker AI model.The company has an AWS Glue Data Catalog that is configured with the S3 bucket as the source. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to create a repository for this data. The solution must ensure that the data stays synchronized during batch training and real-time inference.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Ingest data into SageMaker Feature Store from the S3 bucket. Apply tags and indexes.
choices.B: B. Use Amazon Athena. Create tables by using CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) queries to group data.
choices.C: C. Use AWS Lake Formation. Apply tag-based control on the data.
choices.D: D. Use the Generate Data Insights function in SageMaker Data Wrangler.

question: A hospital is using an ML model to validate x-ray results. The hospital runs a nightly batch inference job. The hospital needs to produce a daily report about model data quality and model performance.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Schedule a monitoring job in Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor. Generate the monitoring results for the model and data.
choices.B: B. Create an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard that includes the metrics for processing steps in the nightly batch inference job. Compare the baseline resource metrics. Share the dashboard link.
choices.C: C. Use AWS Glue DataBrew to create a custom recipe job that uses the Numerical Statistics data quality check for the model file. Generate the results.
choices.D: D. Create a SageMaker AI pipeline that includes a QualityCheck step to run monitoring jobs. Generate the monitoring results for the model and the data.

question: A company needs to ingest data from data sources into Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler. The data sources are Amazon S3, Amazon Redshift, and Snowflake. The ingested data must always be up to date with the latest changes in the source systems.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use direct connections to import data from the data sources into Data Wrangler.
choices.B: B. Use cataloged connections to import data from the data sources into Data Wrangler.
choices.C: C. Use AWS Glue to extract data from the data sources. Use AWS Glue also to import the data directly into Data Wrangler.
choices.D: D. Use AWS Lambda to extract data from the data sources. Use Lambda also to import the data directly into Data Wrangler.

question: An ML engineer is using Amazon SageMaker Canvas to build a custom ML model from an imported dataset. The ML engineer wants the model to make continuous numeric predictions based on 10 years of data.Which metric should the ML engineer use to evaluate the model’s performance?

choices.A: A. Accuracy
choices.B: B. InferenceLatency
choices.C: C. Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
choices.D: D. Root mean square error (RMSE)

question: A company has built, trained, and tuned two new ML models:• Model A detects if a transaction is fraudulent based on the IP address, location, and user credentials. This model will be accessed every time a transaction occurs.• Model B forecasts sales totals for the next month based on historical sales data. This model will be accessed one time each month.The company must deploy both models to production by using Amazon SageMaker AI.Which hosting solution for the models should the company use to meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Host both models in one container behind one real-time endpoint.
choices.B: B. Host Model A with an asynchronous endpoint. Host Model B with a real-time endpoint.
choices.C: C. Host Model A with a real-time endpoint. Use batch transform for Model B.
choices.D: D. Use batch transform for Model A. Host Model B with an asynchronous endpoint.

question: A bank needs to use Amazon SageMaker AI to create an ML model to determine which customers qualify for a new product. The bank must use algorithms that SageMaker AI directly supports. The model must be explainable to the bank’s regulators.Which modeling approach will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Train the model by using the Object2Vec algorithm.
choices.B: B. Train the model by using the linear learner algorithm.
choices.C: C. Train a neural network.
choices.D: D. Train the model by using the k-means algorithm.

question: A company is preparing data to train a new ML model on Amazon SageMaker AI. The data has not been used before for ML training. The data includes duplicates and is missing some values.The company needs to increase the data quality and detect any statistical bias in the data.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use SageMaker Clarify to create data quality rules. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to detect bias.
choices.B: B. Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to create data quality rules. Use SageMaker Clarify to detect bias.
choices.C: C. Use SageMaker Debugger to create data quality rules. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to detect bias.
choices.D: D. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to create data quality rules. Use SageMaker Clarify to detect bias.

question: An ML engineer is building a model to predict house and apartment prices. The model uses three features: Square Meters, Price, and Age of Building. The dataset has 10,000 data rows. The data includes data points for one large mansion and one extremely small apartment.The ML engineer must perform preprocessing on the dataset to ensure that the model produces accurate predictions for the typical house or apartment.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Remove the outliers and perform a log transformation on the Square Meters variable.
choices.B: B. Keep the outliers and perform normalization on the Square Meters variable.
choices.C: C. Remove the outliers and perform one-hot encoding on the Square Meters variable.
choices.D: D. Keep the outliers and perform one-hot encoding on the Square Meters variable.

question: A company is developing an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The company must monitor bias in the model and must display the results on a dashboard. An ML engineer creates a bias monitoring job.How should the ML engineer capture bias metrics to display on the dashboard?

choices.A: A. Capture AWS CloudTrail metrics from SageMaker Clarify.
choices.B: B. Capture Amazon CloudWatch metrics from SageMaker Clarify.
choices.C: C. Capture SageMaker Model Monitor metrics from Amazon EventBridge.
choices.D: D. Capture SageMaker Model Monitor metrics from Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS).

question: A company is using an Amazon SageMaker AI ML model to predict traffic accidents that potholes cause. An ML engineer has configured SageMaker Model Monitor to run as part of a SageMaker AI pipeline. In the MonitoringExecution output, the ML engineer observes several baseline_drift_check violations that are failing the pipeline.What should the ML engineer do to resolve this issue?

choices.A: A. Retrain the model by using a new SageMaker AI training job. Check for errors by using SageMaker Debugger.
choices.B: B. Retrain the model with new training data. Reuse the original baseline in Model Monitor.
choices.C: C. Retrain the model with new training data. Use the new baseline in Model Monitor.
choices.D: D. Rerun the SageMaker AI pipeline after enabling the emit_metrics option in the baseline constraints file.

question: A company uses ML models to predict whether transactions are fraudulent. The company needs to identify as many fraudulent transactions as possible.Which evaluation metric should the company use to evaluate the models to meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. F1 score
choices.B: B. Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
choices.C: C. Precision

question: A recommendation model uses ML and calls an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint to get recommendations. An ML engineer must ensure that the model stays available during an expected increase in user traffic.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Configure auto scaling on the SageMaker AI endpoint.

question: An ML engineer has trained an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The ML engineer determines that the model is overfitting and that the training data contains unnecessary features. question: An ML engineer wants to use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to perform preprocessing on a dataset. The ML engineer wants to use the processed dataset to train a classification model. question: An ML engineer is training a text generation model on Amazon SageMaker AI. choices.A: A. Increase the learning rate and decrease the mini-batch size.

question: A company uses an NFS-based data store to store data for ML training. Linux-based systems access the data store.The company needs a hybrid system to make the shared data store accessible to on-premises servers and Amazon SageMaker AI notebooks that will consume the data. File locking is required for the data producers.Which AWS storage solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use an Amazon S3 bucket to store the data. Use Mountpoint for Amazon S3 to mount the S3 bucket to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.
choices.B: B. Use an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system to store the data. Mount the file system to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.
choices.C: C. Use an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system to store the data. Mount the file system to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.
choices.D: D. Use an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume to store the data. Mount the volume to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.

question: A company needs to analyze a large dataset that is stored in Amazon S3 in Apache Parquet format. choices.A: A. Configure an AWS Glue DataBrew project that connects to the data. Use the DataBrew interactive interface to create a recipe that performs the one-hot encoding transformation. Create a job to apply the transformation and to write the output back to an S3 bucket.
choices.B: B. Configure an AWS Glue Data Catalog table that points to the data. Use Amazon Athena to write SQL commands to perform the one-hot encoding transformation. Configure Athena to write the query results back to an S3 bucket.

question: A company wants to migrate ML models from an on-premises environment to Amazon SageMaker AI. The models are based on the PyTorch algorithm. question: A company uses an Amazon QuickSight dashboard to track the sale prices of sneakers over time. The dashboard aggregates sale prices scraped from many retail websites. question: An ML engineer is using Amazon QuickSight anomaly detection to detect very high or very low machine operating temperatures compared to normal. question: A company runs its ML workflows on an on-premises Kubernetes cluster. The ML workflows include ML services that perform training and inferences for ML models. Each ML service runs from its own standalone Docker image.The company needs to perform a lift and shift from the on-premises Kubernetes cluster to an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster.Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: A. Redesign the ML services to be configured in Kubeflow. Deploy the new Kubeflow managed ML services to the EKS cluster.

question: An ML engineer needs to run intensive model training jobs each month that can take 48 to 72 hours to run. The training jobs can be interrupted and resumed without major issues. choices.B: B. Purchase On-Demand Instances with no commitment.
choices.C: C. Purchase Amazon SageMaker AI Savings Plans.

question: A retail company is creating an AI-powered assistant for customers. The company has a large body of documentation that the assistant needs to use for general inquiries. question: A travel company wants to create an ML model to recommend the next airport destination for its users. The company has collected millions of data records about user location, recent search history on the company’s website, and 2,000 available airports. The data has several categorical features with a target column that is expected to have a high-dimensional sparse matrix.The company needs to use Amazon SageMaker AI built-in algorithms for the model. question: An ML engineer is configuring auto scaling for an inference component of a model that runs behind an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint. question: An ML engineer is using an Amazon SageMaker Studio notebook to train a neural network by creating an estimator. The estimator runs a Python training script that uses Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) on a single instance that has more than one GPU.The ML engineer discovers that the training script is underutilizing GPU resources. question: An ML engineer at an insurance company trains a regression model to predict the number of insurance policy sales each month. choices.A: A. Store the images in an Amazon S3 bucket. Deploy the model on SageMaker AI. Use batch transform jobs for model inference. Use an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue to notify users.

question: A healthcare company uses an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint to host a model that predicts patient readmission risk to hospitals. choices.D: D. Configure auto scaling based on the SageMaker InvocationsPerInstance metric. Configure high-resolution 10-second intervals, and set the default 300-second scale-in cooldown period.

question: An ML engineer is collecting data to train a classification ML model by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The target column can have two possible values: Class A or Class B. question: An ML engineer is building a logistic regression model to predict customer churn for subscription services. question: A company needs to deploy a custom-trained classification ML model on AWS. The model must make near real-time predictions with low latency and must handle variable request volumes.Which solution will meet these requirements?

question: A company runs a neural network model and retrains the model when the performance degrades. The company uses a training job that uses Amazon SageMaker AI distributed data parallelism (DDP). choices.C: C. Increase the number of layers.
choices.D: D. Increase the number of instances.

question: An ML engineer decides to use Amazon SageMaker AI automated model tuning (AMT) for hyperparameter optimization (HPO). The ML engineer requires a tuning strategy that uses regression to slowly and sequentially select the next set of hyperparameters based on previous runs. The strategy must work across small hyperparameter ranges.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Grid search
choices.B: B. Random search
choices.C: C. Bayesian optimization
choices.D: D. Hyperband

question: A healthcare company wants to detect irregularities in patient vital signs that could indicate early signs of a medical condition. The company has an unlabeled dataset that includes patient health records, medication history, and lifestyle changes.Which algorithm and hyperparameter should the company use to meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI XGBoost algorithm. Set max_depth to greater than 100 to regulate tree complexity.
choices.B: B. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI k-means clustering algorithm. Set k to determine the number of clusters.
choices.C: C. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI DeepAR algorithm. Set epochs to the number of training iterations.
choices.D: D. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI Random Cut Forest (RCF) algorithm. Set num_trees to greater than 100. Most Voted

question: A company is training an image classification model on Amazon SageMaker AI to differentiate between images of cats and dogs. The company is using images from a third-party source. The company needs to label the images of cats and dogs by adding the correct label to a metadata summary file that is attached to each image. The company wants to ensure that the dataset includes an equal number of images of cats and dogs.Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Use SageMaker Clarify to check for class imbalance (CI). If the value is close to 1, then the dataset is balanced.
choices.B: B. Use SageMaker Clarify to check for class imbalance (CI). If the value is close to 0, then the dataset is balanced.
choices.C: C. Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to check for difference in proportions of labels (DPL). If the value is close to 1, then the dataset is balanced.
choices.D: D. Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to check for difference in proportions of labels (DPL). If the value is close to -1, then the dataset is balanced.

question: An ML engineer at an entertainment company is refining an ML model to predict audience preferences. As the model learns from data, the ML engineer notices that the model’s performance peaks early and then begins to gradually decline. The ML engineer must prevent the performance degradation after initial success.Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Increase the number of layers.
choices.B: B. Implement early stopping.
choices.C: C. Add more neurons to each layer to capture complex patterns.
choices.D: D. Examine model bias and variance to understand performance issues.

question: An ML engineer is tuning an image classification model that shows poor performance on one of two available classes during prediction. Analysis reveals that the images whose class the model performed poorly on represent an extremely small fraction of the whole training dataset.The ML engineer must improve the model’s performance.Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Optimize for accuracy. Use image augmentation on the less common images to generate new samples.
choices.B: B. Optimize for F1 score. Use image augmentation on the less common images to generate new samples.
choices.C: C. Optimize for accuracy. Use Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) on the less common images to generate new samples.
choices.D: D. Optimize for F1 score. Use Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) on the less common images to generate new samples.

question: A healthcare analytics company wants to segment patients into groups that have similar risk factors to develop personalized treatment plans. The company has a dataset that includes patient health records, medication history, and lifestyle changes. The company must identify the appropriate algorithm to determine the number of groups by using hyperparameters.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI XGBoost algorithm. Set max_depth to control tree complexity for risk groups.
choices.B: B. Use the Amazon SageMaker k-means clustering algorithm. Set k to specify the number of clusters.
choices.C: C. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI DeepAR algorithm. Set epochs to determine the number of training iterations for risk groups.
choices.D: D. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI Random Cut Forest (RCF) algorithm. Set a contamination hyperparameter for risk anomaly groups.

question: An ML engineer needs to develop an AI assistant by using Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) in Amazon Bedrock. The company stores a collection of PDF text files in an Amazon S3 bucket. The ML engineer must create an Amazon Bedrock knowledge base to process the PDF files and to store the processed files in a vector store.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use the latest version of Amazon Titan Text Premier to perform document embedding.
choices.B: B. Use the latest version of Mistral 7B Instruct to perform instruction tuning.
choices.C: C. Use the latest version of Anthropic Claude Sonnet to perform instruction tuning.
choices.D: D. Use Cohere Embed Multilingual to perform document embedding.

question: An ML engineer needs to build a processing pipeline to identify and remove personally identifiable information (PII) from petabytes of unstructured data. The ML engineer will use the processed data to train ML models in Amazon SageMaker AI.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use the Apache Spark-based serverless engine from AWS Glue interactive sessions. Use the Detect PII transform feature to identify and remove the PII data. Most Voted
choices.B: B. Use AWS Glue Data Wrangler within Amazon SageMaker Canvas to detect and remove the PII.
choices.C: C. Use the Amazon SageMaker Clarify API to detect and mask the PII data.
choices.D: D. Use the DetectEntities API action in Amazon Comprehend to identify and remove the PII data.

question: A company develops a recommendation model and hosts the model on an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint. The model uses the SageMaker AI endpoint to perform near real-time inference to deliver personalized product recommendations to customers based on browsing history, purchase records, and in-app user interactions.After a major marketing campaign, the company observes a sharp drop in the model’s performance. The company needs a solution to proactively monitor, detect, and validate model performance before future marketing campaigns.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use SageMaker Clarify to analyze changes in feature distribution. Configure SageMaker Model Monitor for near real-time input validation.
choices.B: B. Use Amazon CloudWatch dashboards to monitor endpoint metrics. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to track feature attribution.
choices.C: C. Use SageMaker Clarify for bias detection. Set up Amazon CloudWatch alarms to monitor model latency.
choices.D: D. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to monitor constraints. Use Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights to analyze error patterns.

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to create a classification model to categorize the company’s sales performance for each month of the previous 20 years on a scale from 1 to 5. The dataset includes fields for month, sales region, regional aggregate sales, and the number of stores in each sales region. The company notices that during two months of every year, the aggregate sales values are unexpectedly high. The company performs one-hot encoding on all non-numerical features in the training and validation datasets. The company uses the training dataset to train the classification model. When the company evaluates the model against the validation dataset, the results are less accurate than expected.The company must improve the model’s accuracy on the validation dataset.Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Remove records that include outliers across all features.
choices.B: B. Use a stratified split on the month and sales region features.
choices.C: C. Perform normalization on the aggregate sales feature.
choices.D: D. Perform normalization on the aggregate sales feature for each sales region.

question: A company is creating an ML model to identify defects in a product. The company has gathered a dataset and has stored the dataset in TIFF format in Amazon S3. The dataset contains 200 images in which the most common defects are visible. The dataset also contains 1,800 images in which there is no defect visible.An ML engineer trains the model and notices poor performance in some classes. The ML engineer identifies a class imbalance problem in the dataset.What should the ML engineer do to solves this problem?

choices.A: A. Use a few hundred images and Amazon Rekognition Custom Labels to train a new model.
choices.B: B. Undersample the 200 images in which the most common defects are visible.
choices.C: C. Oversample the 200 images in which the most common defects are visible.
choices.D: D. Use all 2,000 images and Amazon Rekognition Custom Labels to train a new model.

question: An ML engineer is building an ML model in Amazon SageMaker AI. The ML engineer needs to load historical data directly from Amazon S3, Amazon Athena, and Snowflake into SageMaker AI.Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Use AWS Glue DataBrew to import the data into SageMaker AI.
choices.B: B. Build a pipeline in SageMaker Pipelines to process the data. Use AWS DataSync to load the processed data into SageMaker AI.
choices.C: C. Create a feature store in SageMaker Feature Store. Use an Apache Spark connector to Feature Store to access the data.
choices.D: D. Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to query and import the data. Most Voted

question: An ML engineer has developed a custom scikit-learn script to transform tabular data to train a regression model. The ML engineer wants to use Amazon SageMaker AI to prepare data and to train and evaluate the model.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Create an AWS Lambda function to run the custom script. Load the processed data into SageMaker AI to train the model.
choices.B: B. Use the SKLearnProcessor class within the SageMaker Python SDK to run the custom script.
choices.C: C. Apply the HuggingFaceProcessor class within the SageMaker Python SDK to run the custom script.
choices.D: D. Use AWS Glue ETL custom recipes to perform data transformations. Load the processed data into SageMaker AI to train the model.

question: A company is developing an application that reads animal descriptions from user prompts and generates images based on the information from the prompts. The application reads a message from an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Then the application uses Amazon Titan Image Generator on Amazon Bedrock to generate an image based on the information in the message. Finally, the application removes the message from SQS queue.Which IAM permissions should the company assign to the application’s IAM role? (Choose two.)

choices.A: A. Allow the bedrock:InvokeModel action for the Amazon Titan Image Generator resource.
choices.B: B. Allow the bedrock:Get* action for the Amazon Titan Image Generator resource.
choices.C: C. Allow the sqs:ReceiveMessage action and the sqs:DeleteMessage action for the SQS queue resource.
choices.D: D. Allow the sqs:GetQueueAttributes action and the sqs:DeleteMessage action for the SQS queue resource.
choices.E: E. Allow the sagemaker:PutRecord* action for the Amazon Titan Image Generator resource.

question: An ML engineer is setting up a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipeline for an ML workflow in Amazon SageMaker AI. The pipeline needs to automate model re-training, testing, and deployment whenever new data is uploaded to an Amazon S3 bucket. New data files are approximately 10 GB in size. The ML engineer wants to track model versions for auditing.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use AWS CodePipeline. Amazon S3, and AWS CodeBuild to retrain and deploy the model automatically and to track model versions.
choices.B: B. Use SageMaker Pipelines with the SageMaker Model Registry to orchestrate model training and version tracking.
choices.C: C. Create an AWS Lambda function to re-train and deploy the model. Use Amazon EventBridge to invoke the Lambda function. Reference the Lambda logs to track model versions.
choices.D: D. Use SageMaker AI notebook instances to manually re-train and deploy the model when needed. Reference AWS CloudTrail logs to track model versions.

question: An ML engineering team is spread across multiple locations. When the lead ML engineer opens an Amazon SageMaker Al notebook, the ML engineer does not see the latest merged notebook made by other team members from a Git repository.The lead ML engineer must see the latest SageMaker AI notebook updates.Which solution will meet this requirement?

choices.A: A. Run the !git pull origin master command.
choices.B: B. Run the !git commit command.
choices.C: C. Run the !git push origin master command.
choices.D: D. Run the !git branch command.

question: An ML engineer is preparing data for binary classification modeling. The ML engineer runs an Amazon SageMaker Clarify processing job. The bias report shows that the difference in proportions of labels (DPL) score is 0.4. The ML engineer wants the occurrence of labels across classes to be equal. The ML engineer decides to use SageMaker Data Wrangler to balance the data.What should the ML engineer do to most of the data to meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use random oversampling to increase the DPL score.
choices.B: B. Use random undersampling to increase the DPL score
choices.C: C. Use random oversampling to reduce the DPL score.
choices.D: D. Use random undersampling to reduce the DPL score.

question: An ML engineer is using Amazon SageMaker AI to train a deep learning model. During testing, the model shows high variance. The ML engineer needs to adjust a hyperparameter to improve generalization without significantly increasing training time.Which solution will MOST improve the model generalization?

choices.A: A. Increase the number of layers in the neural network.
choices.B: B. Reduce the learning rate of the optimizer.
choices.C: C. Increase the L2 regularization parameter
choices.D: D. Increase the batch size significantly.

question: An ML engineer is running a hyperparameter tuning job in Amazon SageMaker AI. The hyperparameter tuning job will train an XGBoost model on a tabular dataset that has millions of rows.Each individual training job is configured to use up to 8 GPU instances, which leads to high costs if too many jobs run concurrently. The ML engineer wants to ensure that no more than five training jobs run in parallel at any time while still exploring all hyperparameter configurations.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Set the resource limit for the MaxNumberOfTrainingJobs parameter to 5 in the hyperparameter tuning job.
choices.B: B. Set the resource limit for the MaxParallelTrainingJobs parameter to 5 in the hyperparameter tuning job.
choices.C: C. Set the stopping condition for the MaxRuntimeInSeconds parameter to 5 in the hyperparameter tuning job.
choices.D: D. Set the resource limit for the MaxNumberOfTrainingJobs parameter to 5 inside the script in the hyperparameter training job.

question: A company is developing an ML model to forecast future values based on time series data. The dataset includes historical measurements collected at regular intervals and categorical features. The model needs to predict future values based on past patterns and trends.Which algorithm and hyperparameters should the company use to develop the model?

choices.A: A. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI XGBoost algorithm. Set the scale_pos_weight hyperparameter to adjust for class imbalance.

choices.D: D. Use the Amazon SageMaker AI Random Cut Forest (RCF) algorithm with contamination to set the expected proportion of anomalies.

question: A company uses an Amazon EMR cluster to run a data ingestion process for an ML model. An ML engineer notices that the processing time is increasing.Which solution will reduce the processing time MOST cost-effectively?

choices.A: A. Use Spot Instances to increase the number of primary nodes.
choices.B: B. Use Spot Instances to increase the number of core nodes.
choices.C: C. Use Spot Instances to increase the number of task nodes.
choices.D: D. Use On-Demand Instances to increase the number of core nodes.

question: An ML engineer is designing an AI-powered traffic management system to adjust traffic lights during predicted congestion. The system must use near real-time inference to generate predictions to help prevent traffic collisions. The system must use a batch processing pipeline to perform historical analysis of the predictions to continuously refine and improve the model. The historical analysis will take several hours to evaluate how well the predictions correlate with actual outcomes. The system must be able to scale inference endpoints appropriately to meet demand.Which combination of solutions will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

choices.A: A. Use Amazon SageMaker real-time inference endpoints. Configure the endpoints to scale automatically based on a target tracking scaling policy that uses the metric ConcurrentInvocationsPerinstance.
choices.B: B. Configure reserved concurrency for AWS Lambda functions to process streaming data. Use Lambda SnapStart to connect the Lambda functions to Amazon SageMaker real-time endpoints to support near real-time traffic predictions.
choices.C: C. Configure an Amazon SageMaker Processing job for batch analysis of historical prediction data. Use Amazon EventBridge to schedule the job to run daily. Allow several hours for in-depth analysis to refine and improve the traffic management model.
choices.D: D. Use an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group to host containers to support the batch analysis of historical prediction data. Configure scaling based on Amazon CloudWatch metrics to analyze historical traffic patterns and model performance over multiple hours.
choices.E: E. Use an AWS Lambda function to perform the historical analysis. Use Amazon EventBridge to invoke the Lambda function.

question: A company wants to launch a new website feature that predicts home prices based on user-supplied home attributes. choices.A: A. Create a larger training dataset that includes more real-world data. Retrain the model.
choices.B: B. Increase the value of the num_round hyperparameter.
choices.C: C. Change the eval_metric hyperparameter from Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) to Error.

question: A company is developing an ML model for a customer. The company will train the model on data that is in an Amazon S3 bucket in the customer’s AWS account, named Account A. choices.C: C. Create the S3 bucket policy in Account B. Attach the IAM policy to an IAM role that SageMaker AI uses in Account A.
choices.D: D. Create the S3 bucket policy in Account B. Attach the IAM policy to an IAM role that SageMaker AI uses in Account B.

question: A company is performing A/B testing on a model that provides product recommendations. choices.D: D. The latency of model inference

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to build an ML model to predict customer behavior. The company needs to explain the bias in the model to an auditor. The explanation must focus on demographics data of the customers.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.B: B. Use AWS Glue DataBrew to create a job to detect drift in the model's data quality. Send the job output to the auditor.
choices.C: C. Use Amazon Quick Suite (previously known as Amazon QuickSight) integration with SageMaker AI to generate a bias report from Quick Suite. Send the report to the auditor.
choices.D: D. Use Amazon CloudWatch metrics from the SageMaker AI namespace to create a bias dashboard. Share the dashboard with the auditor.

question: A digital media entertainment company needs real-time video content moderation to ensure compliance during live streaming events.Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

choices.A: A. Use Amazon Rekognition and AWS Lambda to extract and analyze the metadata from the videos’ image frames.
choices.B: B. Use Amazon Rekognition and a large language model (LLM) hosted on Amazon Bedrock to extract and analyze the metadata from the videos’ image frames.

question: An ML engineer is working on a project to predict customer churn. The dataset contains customer information and is stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to train a face detection ML model. The company must manage the encryption control. Only authorized employees must be able to view the data.Which solution will meet these requirements?

choices.A: A. Use SageMaker AI IAM roles and policies to control access. Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) for encryption.
choices.B: B. Use AWS PrivateLink for SageMaker AI to control access. Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) for encryption.

question: A company is evaluating options to deploy an ML model that will be used to make predictions. choices.A: A. Deploy the model on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer.
choices.B: B. Deploy the model to Amazon SageMaker AI real-time inference endpoints.
choices.C: C. Deploy the model on Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) containers that run on AWS Fargate.
choices.D: D. Deploy the model on Kubernetes nodes on an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster.

question: An ML engineer uses A/B testing to dynamically select recommendation models. The models are deployed on Amazon SageMaker AI endpoints. choices.B: B. Configure Amazon CloudWatch dashboards with AWS Lambda log processors.
choices.C: C. Enable AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) Access Analyzer to track SageMaker AI endpoint metrics.
choices.D: D. Deploy AWS Trusted Advisor checks on SageMaker AI endpoint instances.

question: An ML engineer at a credit card company used Amazon SageMaker AI to build and deploy a new ML model. The model was trained on a copy of transactions from the production environment. The copy of transactions contained only a few fraudulent transactions.After deployment to production, the model is underperforming.What should the ML engineer do to improve the model’s performance?

choices.A: A. Retrain the model with a different built-in algorithm that is available in SageMaker AI.
choices.B: B. Use random undersampling in SageMaker Data Wrangler to randomly reduce the majority class samples. Retrain the model.

question: An ML engineer is importing a custom model from the Hugging Face Hub into Amazon Bedrock. The ML engineer wants to use the model with the Amazon Bedrock API in an agentic AI application.Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

choices.A: A. Import the model for deployment by using Amazon Bedrock On-Demand mode.
choices.B: B. Convert the model to a quantized format. Import the quantized model after conversion.
choices.C: C. Import the model directly by using the Amazon SageMaker AI SDK in the agentic AI application.

question: A company is developing a classification model to predict profitability. The company wants to incorporate new unstructured data that the company bought from another company. choices.D: D. Store the new data in a local file on each data scientist’s laptop to ensure that only that specific data scientists can access the data.

question: A company is developing ML models by using PyTorch and TensorFlow estimators with Amazon SageMaker AI. An ML engineer configures the SageMaker AI estimator and now needs to initiate a training job that uses a training dataset.Which SageMaker AI SDK method can initiate the training job?

choices.B: B. create_model method

question: An ML engineer is preparing a dataset of medical records to train an ML model. The model will predict the likelihood of patients developing a specific disease. choices.A: A. Select a random sample of records. Encode the categorical features. Load the pre-processed data into the file system.

choices.A: A. Increase the initial learning rate.
choices.B: B. Decrease the initial learning rate.

choices.D: D. Decrease the total number of epochs.

question: A company wants to use large language models (LLMs) that are supported by Amazon Bedrock to develop a chat interface for the company’s internal technical documentation. The company stores the documentation as dozens of text files that are several megabytes in total size. The company updates the text files often.Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

choices.A: A. Create a new LLM on Amazon Bedrock. Train the new LLM on the original dataset and the company documentation. Make the new model available in Bedrock for calls from the chat interface.

choices.D: D. Upload all the text files to an Amazon Bedrock knowledge base. Use the knowledge base to provide context when the chat interface makes calls to Amazon Bedrock.

question: An ML engineer is using AWS CodeDeploy to deploy new versions of containers that perform inference to an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) compute platform. The ML engineer needs to define a deployment configuration to shift traffic to the updated ECS task set. The deployment configuration must shift 10% of traffic to the updated containers in the first increment. choices.A: A. CodeDeployDefault.LambdaLinear10PercentEvery10Minutes

question: A company uses an ML model to recommend videos to users. The model is deployed on Amazon SageMaker AI. The model performed well initially after deployment, but the model’s performance has degraded over time.Which solution can the company use to identify model drift in the future?

choices.A: A. Create a monitoring job in SageMaker Model Monitor. Then create a baseline from the training dataset.

choices.C: C. Create a baseline by using a built-in rule in SageMaker Clarify. Monitor the drift in Amazon CloudWatch.
